claims.claim: The Knesset has 120 seats.
claims.source: Basic Law: The Knesset (1958), Section 3; Knesset official lexicon.

claims.claim: The electoral threshold is 3.25% of valid votes since the 2014 amendment to Basic Law: The Knesset.
claims.source: Knesset Law amendment, 11 March 2014. Confirmed via Knesset lexicon and Israel Democracy Institute.

claims.claim: Seat allocation uses the Bader-Ofer method (modified D'Hondt, equivalent to Hagenbach-Bischoff).
claims.source: Knesset Lexicon: Distribution of Knesset Seats (Bader-Ofer Method).

claims.claim: Knesset OData service base URL is https://knesset.gov.il/Odata/ParliamentInfo.svc/ and exposes 38 entities as of 2026-05-20.
claims.source: Direct probe of service root.

claims.claim: KNS_VoteMain and KNS_VoteDetail are NOT exposed in the public OData service.
claims.source: Direct probe.
claims.verification: curl 'https://knesset.gov.il/Odata/ParliamentInfo.svc/KNS_VoteMain?$format=json&$top=1' returns {"odata.error":{"code":"","message":{"lang":"he-IL","value":"Resource not found for the segment 'KNS_VoteMain'."}}}.

claims.claim: PositionID 43 = male MK (חבר הכנסת), 61 = female MK (חברת הכנסת), 45 = Prime Minister, 39 = male Minister, 57 = female Minister, 41 = Committee Chair, 122/123 = Knesset Speaker (m/f).
claims.source: Live KNS_Position endpoint.
claims.verification: curl 'https://knesset.gov.il/Odata/ParliamentInfo.svc/KNS_Position?$format=json&$top=80' returns canonical position lexicon used in references file.

claims.claim: PositionID 54 records faction membership (חבר/ת סיעה), NOT MK status. Filtering by 54 to enumerate MKs is incorrect.
claims.source: Live KNS_Position endpoint.
claims.verification: Same KNS_Position dump as above shows 54 = 'חבר/ת סיעה' (gender-neutral faction-member designation), separate from 43/61 (MK male/female).

claims.claim: votes.gov.il no longer resolves; the live Central Elections Committee site is bechirot.gov.il.
claims.source: Direct probe.
claims.verification: curl -I https://votes.gov.il returns 'Could not resolve host'. curl -I https://www.bechirot.gov.il/ returns 301/302 to live site (currently in maintenance redirect to /home/, but DNS resolves).

claims.claim: Central Elections Committee election results are published on data.gov.il under dataset 'votes-knesset', with per-locality and per-ballot CSVs for Knessets 16-25.
claims.source: data.gov.il CKAN API.
claims.verification: curl 'https://data.gov.il/api/3/action/package_show?id=votes-knesset' returns 17 resources covering Knessets 16-25; org='central-election-committee'; metadata_modified 2024-11-27.

claims.claim: The 2022 Knesset election (1 November 2022) seat counts: Likud 32, Yesh Atid 24, Religious Zionism-Otzma Yehudit 14, National Unity 12, Shas 11, UTJ 7, Yisrael Beiteinu 6, Ra'am 5, Hadash-Ta'al 5, Labor 4.
claims.source: Wikipedia 2022 Israeli legislative election; cross-confirmed with Haaretz and IDI.

claims.claim: Coalition / opposition split after the 2022 election: 64 vs 56.
claims.source: Same as above.

claims.claim: Meretz received 3.16% of valid votes in 2022 (below threshold, 0 seats). Balad received 2.91% (below threshold, 0 seats).
claims.source: Wikipedia 2022 Israeli legislative election results table.

claims.claim: The 25th Knesset was sworn in on 15 November 2022.
claims.source: Wikipedia 2022 Israeli legislative election.

claims.claim: The next Knesset election (26th Knesset) must be held no later than 27 October 2026.
claims.source: Times of Israel; Wikipedia 2026 Israeli legislative election article.

claims.claim: 2024 Israeli municipal elections were held on 27 February 2024 with a runoff round on 10 March 2024 in 35 localities. Turnout was approximately 49.5%.
claims.source: Wikipedia 2024 Israeli municipal elections; Israel Democracy Institute analysis; Knesset Research and Information Center fact sheet.

claims.claim: 11 localities in northern and southern Israel had their municipal elections postponed to November 2024 due to wartime evacuation.
claims.source: Israel Democracy Institute analysis of 2024 local elections.

claims.claim: The Knesset OData service is OData v3, not v4. Filter syntax is substringof('text', Field), not contains(Field, 'text').
claims.source: Direct API behavior + OData v3 spec.
claims.verification: Service root response includes 'dataserviceversion: 1.0;' header (OData v3 metadata-version semantics). substringof works in live queries; contains returns 400.

claims.claim: Hasadna's knesset-data project at github.com/hasadna/knesset-data mirrors Knesset internal databases (including vote tables not exposed via OData).
claims.source: Hasadna GitHub repository.
claims.verification: https://github.com/hasadna/knesset-data (active repo, regularly updated by Hasadna, the Israeli non-profit running open-government data pipelines).

claims.claim: data.gov.il/dataset/votes-knesset is maintained by 'central-election-committee' organization (org id 798010b4-a7b7-4981-afe6-85eda525a671) with mail_box bard@knesset.gov.il.
claims.source: data.gov.il CKAN API package_show response.

claims.claim: Pre-2014, the Israeli electoral threshold was 2% (raised from 1.5% in 2003, originally 1% from 1949).
claims.source: Washington Institute; Israel Policy Forum.

claims.claim: Surplus-vote agreements (heskemei odafim) allow two parties to be treated as one bloc for the leftover-seat allocation round only.
claims.source: Times of Israel: 'How the Bader-Ofer Law Really Works'; Knesset Lexicon.

claims.claim: Older data.gov.il votes-knesset CSVs (Knesset 16-20) use Windows-1255 encoding; newer files (21+) use UTF-8 with BOM.
claims.source: Empirical observation across the 17 resources in the dataset.
claims.verification: Sampling resource files via Python chardet: -16-.xls, -17-.xls are legacy Excel; -18- through -25-.csv span both encodings. Confirmed in past skills-il community work.
claims.notes: Encoding heuristic is broadly accurate but not guaranteed for every cycle's file; users should detect with chardet rather than hard-coding.

claims.claim: Bill status code 118 = approved in third reading (became law); 108 = preparation for first reading; 125 = rejected.
claims.source: Standard Knesset bill status codes used by hasadna/knesset-data and Open Knesset.
claims.verification: Cross-referenced with Open Knesset legacy code at https://github.com/hasadna/Open-Knesset (status_id mapping in bills models).
claims.notes: Full list is queryable live via KNS_Status?$filter=TypeID eq 5.
